grounding_note: Expansion batch: more alive/deceased, trust-titling, and fiduciary-conduct disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: I need someone to help me sort out my father's affairs. The paperwork alone fills a table.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > General (Wills/Trusts/Estates) || Wills & Trusts > Probate
scenarios.hidden_fact: Dad died in February — there's a will naming me executor, and the court process hasn't been started.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He passed in February — the will names me executor and nothing's been filed yet.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the father is deceased and administration is needed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Dad's alive — he's 88 and wants everything organized and updated while he can still direct it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's alive — 88 and wanting everything organized while he can direct it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Alive/deceased again with a different voice; the intake never states it unprompted.

scenarios.opening_query: My sister was in charge of Mom's money for the last five years and the numbers don't add up.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mom is still alive — sister used the POA to move $200,000 into her own accounts while Mom's Alzheimer's worsened, and we need it stopped and recovered now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Mom's still alive — my sister used the POA to move $200,000 to herself as Mom's Alzheimer's worsened. It has to be stopped and recovered.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the elder is living and exploitation is ongoing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Mom passed in the spring — the accounting my sister filed as trustee doesn't add up, and we're contesting it in the estate case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Mom passed in spring — sister's trustee accounting doesn't add up and we're contesting it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Living-elder exploitation (urgent protective) vs post-death fiduciary contest.

scenarios.opening_query: Grandma's house went to auction before any of us grandkids even knew what was happening.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Real Property > Foreclosure
scenarios.hidden_fact: The auction is a reverse-mortgage foreclosure sale set for next month — it hasn't happened yet, and we're told heirs can still redeem or sell first.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a reverse-mortgage foreclosure sale set for next month — it hasn't happened, and heirs can apparently still redeem or sell first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the sale is pending foreclosure with redemption options
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'auction' was the estate's own sale by the administrator — our issue is we were never notified as heirs.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the estate administrator's own sale — we heirs were never notified.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 412 nearly verbatim (grandparents' abandoned house auctioned).

scenarios.opening_query: My husband and I need our wills done. We keep putting it off and we're not getting younger.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > General (Wills/Trusts/Estates) || Wills & Trusts > Living Trusts
scenarios.hidden_fact: We own rental property in three states — everyone says that means a revocable living trust to avoid three separate probates, not just wills.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We own rentals in three states — apparently that means a living trust to avoid three probates, not just wills.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether multi-state property makes trust-based planning the need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's simple — one house, two kids, everything split evenly; basic wills and directives.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's simple — one house, two kids, even split. Basic wills.
scenarios.vetting_note: Asset-geography fact selects the trust-planning label.

scenarios.opening_query: The nursing home is threatening to discharge my mother because her money has run out.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Long-term Care Issues || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her Medicaid application was denied for 'excess resources' that don't exist — a bank error double-counted one account, and the appeal deadline is in three weeks.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her Medicaid was denied for 'excess resources' that don't exist — a bank error double-counted an account, and the appeal deadline is three weeks out.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a benefits denial appeal is the immediate need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She hasn't applied yet — we need her remaining assets structured so she qualifies before the private-pay money is gone.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She hasn't applied — we need her assets structured to qualify before the money runs out.
scenarios.vetting_note: Denied-appeal vs plan-to-qualify at the nursing-home cliff; both labels distinct from long-term-care planning.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother won't show anyone the trust our parents set up, and he's the one running it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Living Trusts || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Both parents have passed — as beneficiaries we have a legal right to the trust document and accountings, and he's refused written demands for a year. We're ready to compel him in court.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Both parents are gone — as beneficiaries we're entitled to the document and accountings, and he's refused written demands for a year. We want to compel him.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether beneficiary-rights enforcement against the trustee is ripe
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Our parents are alive and competent — it's their trust to keep private for now; what we really want is a family meeting about the succession plan.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Our parents are alive and competent — it's their private trust. We really just need the succession conversation.
scenarios.vetting_note: Beneficiary rights vest at death; the alive/deceased fact controls whether there's any case at all.

scenarios.opening_query: I was told I'm in my uncle's will but the family is acting strange and nobody will give me details.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: I got the probate notice — a 'new will' signed six weeks before he died cuts me out entirely, when he could no longer recognize people; I want to contest it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The probate notice shows a 'new will' from six weeks before he died — when he couldn't recognize people — cutting me out. I want to contest it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a late-made will warrants a capacity/undue-influence contest
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No foul play — the estate just hasn't been opened, and I want to know my rights as a named beneficiary in the process.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing shady — the estate just hasn't been opened and I want to know my rights in the process.
scenarios.vetting_note: Deathbed-will facts convert beneficiary curiosity into a will contest.

scenarios.opening_query: My stepmother is still living in Dad's house a year after he passed and nothing is moving.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Real Property > Litigation || Wills & Trusts > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Probate actually closed — the house was distributed to me and my brother outright, and she simply refuses to leave; we need an ejectment case, not estate work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Probate closed — the house is legally mine and my brother's now, and she won't leave. We need an ejectment case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether title has passed making it a property-possession suit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing was ever filed — no probate, no executor, and she's collecting rent from the basement tenant meanwhile.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No probate was ever filed — no executor — and she's collecting the basement tenant's rent meanwhile.
scenarios.vetting_note: Closed-vs-unopened estate flips between property litigation and probate administration.
